message:
test(simulation): add many-core phase-utilization responsiveness gate scenario

Extend the search-asset simulation harness with a fifth scenario that
exercises the many-core responsiveness gate — the invariant that a
segment-farm rebuild must drive higher phase-utilization than the
legacy serial replay path while still yielding to foreground pressure.

`many_core_responsiveness_gate_persists_phase_utilization_evidence`:
- Five-beat LoadScript
  (idle:legacy_serial_baseline / idle:segment_farm_build /
   busy:foreground_probe / loaded:settle_pressure / idle:fully_settled)
  so each turn lands on a distinct capacity regime.
- ContentionPlan with five turns:
    1. LexicalRepair/record_serial_baseline — capture the legacy
       serial replay's per-phase utilization as a baseline.
       Snapshot records CPU-% per stage, aggregate wall-clock, and
       the per-core histogram so the downstream rollout gate has
       a comparable reference.
    2. LexicalRepair/record_segment_farm — segment-farm path's
       utilization, same schema. Must be strictly higher than the
       baseline in at least one phase and must satisfy a minimum
       min_utilization_pct floor.
    3. ForegroundSearch/probe_responsiveness — probe
       responsiveness while segment-farm is active. Foreground
       latency snapshot must come in under the p95 budget.
    4. LexicalRepair/pause_settle_under_pressure — loaded-regime
       beat; segment-farm pauses to yield. Snapshot records the
       pause reason and the pre-pause queue depth.
    5. LexicalRepair/rollout_verdict — evidence-backed rollout
       verdict comparing baseline vs segment-farm utilization,
       pressure behaviour, and p95 latency. Assertions check the
       verdict records `pass` for all three gates.

Complements the rollout-gate / cleanup-quarantine / scheduler
pause-resume scenarios landed earlier in the same branch; the
harness now covers the four invariant classes the downstream
rollout planner has to verify.

`.beads/issues.jsonl` + `.beads/last-touched` bump from br sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
